nodes.context: Colombian drug trafficking neo-paramilitary group involved in the Colombian armed conflict.
Armed, paramilitary group that partially demobilized in 2009; former leaders joined Libertadores del Vichada.
Rival group to Libertadores del Vichada, formed from ERPAC remnants.
Group formed by reuniting Libertadores del Vichada and Meta Block under Mauricio Pachón Rozo.
Colombian rebel group that fought battles against Libertadores del Vichada.

edges.evidence_quote: The group was led by Martín Farfán Díaz González, alias 'Pijarbey,' a former leader of the neo-paramilitary group ERPAC.
edges.context: Libertadores del Vichada's leader was a former leader of ERPAC, indicating a successor relationship.

edges.evidence_quote: the remaining 600 were believed to have joined Libertadores de Vichada or the rivalling Meta Block.
edges.context: Libertadores del Vichada and Meta Block are described as rivals, both formed from ERPAC remnants.

edges.evidence_quote: the two blocs were in the hands of the drug trafficker Mauricio Pachón Rozo, also known as 'Puntilla', reunited the two groups; This time with a new name: 'Los Puntilleros'
edges.context: Libertadores del Vichada and Meta Block were reunited under Los Puntilleros, making Libertadores del Vichada a predecessor.

edges.evidence_quote: Los Libertadores del Vichada have frequently fought battles against the FARC rebel group in eastern Colombia.
edges.context: Libertadores del Vichada fought battles against the FARC.
